head.text: Temporal pains with double vision, impaired sight; brilliant eyes, flushed face. Senile vertigo

stomach_abdomen.text: Nausea with special disgust for alcohol and so aids in treatment of dipsomania. Seven drops of tincture

female.text: Menorrhagia; uterine hæmorrhage; uterus heavily congested. Aching pain through hips and thighs during climacteric

respiratory.text: Dyspnœa, especially on ascending. Lungs congested. Œdema of lungs. Bronchial and cardiac asthma

chest_heart.text: Pulse quickened. Heart's action weak, rapid irregular, due to muscular debility; and insufficiency. Cardiac pain

dosing.notes: Tincture and 6x potency. In more acute cases, five to ten drops of the tincture three times a day

keynotes.text: color="#800000">STROPHANTHUS HISPIDUS Kombe-seed Strophanthus is a muscle poison; it increases the contractile power of all striped muscles. Acts on the heart; increasing the systole and diminishes the rapidity. May be used with advantage to tone the heart, and run off dropsical accumulations. In small doses for weak heart; it feels enlarged. In mitral regurgitation, where œdema and dropsy have supervened (Digit). Strophanthus occasions no gastric distress, has no cumulative effects, is a greater diuretic, and is safer for the aged, as it does not affect the vaso-motors. In pneumonia and in severe prostration from hæmorrhage after operations and acute diseases. After the long use of stimulants; irritable heart of tobacco-smokers. Arterio-sclerosis; rigid arteries of aged. Restores tone to a brittle tissue, especially of the heart muscle and valves. Especially useful in failing compensation dependent upon fatty heart. Hives. Anæmia with palpitation and breathlessness. Exophthalmia goitre. Corpulent persons.
